properties.name: Downtown Austin
properties.description: High-rise downtown core with state government, offices, entertainment districts and Lady Bird Lake waterfront.

properties.name: Hyde Park
properties.description: Austin's first streetcar suburb from the 1890s, now a dense mix of historic bungalows, apartments and small businesses north of UT.

properties.name: Travis Heights
properties.description: Hilly South Austin neighborhood of 1920s–40s homes and greenbelts just east of South Congress and above Lady Bird Lake.

properties.name: Zilker
properties.description: South-central neighborhood next to Zilker Park and Barton Springs, combining 1920s bungalows with newer homes and a strong outdoor and nightlife scene.

properties.name: Mueller
properties.description: 711-acre redevelopment of the former municipal airport into a dense, walkable, mixed-income urban village with housing, offices, retail and parks.

properties.name: The Domain
properties.description: 303-acre high-density mixed-use district in north Austin often called a second downtown, combining tech offices, apartments and upscale retail.

properties.name: Circle C Ranch
properties.description: 1980s master-planned community in southwest Austin with thousands of homes, parks, an 18-hole golf course and extensive greenbelt access.

properties.name: Easton Park
properties.description: Large southeast Austin planned unit development with thousands of planned homes, extensive parks, trails and community amenities near McKinney Falls.
